description: The Department for Work and Pensions (DWP) is responsible for welfare, pensions and child maintenance policy. As the UK’s biggest public service department it administers the State Pension and a range of working age, disability and ill health benefits to around 20 million claimants and customers. DWP is a ministerial department, supported by 15 agencies and public bodies .

details.body:
The Department for Work and Pensions (DWP) is responsible for welfare, pensions and child maintenance policy. As the UK’s biggest public service department it administers the State Pension and a range of working age, disability and ill health benefits to around 20 million claimants and customers.

DWP is a ministerial department, supported by 15 agencies and public bodies.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The Timms Review is today announcing a wider programme of evidence-gathering and public engagement, giving disabled people, Disabled People’s Organisations, carers, practitioners, clinicians, researchers and other experts more ways to share evidence and experience.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Have your say: PIP review launches engagement programme for disabled people to get involved

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Over 20 million workers are set to get more from every pound they save towards retirement thanks to the passing into law of historic pensions legislation today, Wednesday 29 April 2026.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Retirement boost of £29,000 awaits millions as landmark Pension Schemes Act becomes law

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Training tackles harmful assumptions about disability and ensures staff make practical, meaningful adjustments for the people they serve.
ordered_featured_documents.title: DWP puts disabled people first: Thousands of health staff complete landmark autism and learning disabilities training

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Young people across the United Kingdom are set to benefit after McDonald’s becomes the latest major employer to support the Government’s Youth Guarantee and launches the biggest work experience programme in the country.
ordered_featured_documents.title: McDonald’s is supporting the government's drive to get young people earning or learning

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Tens of thousands of unpaid carers affected by confusing guidance on their earnings are set to have their debts reduced, cancelled, or refunded, in a major reassessment of cases launched by the government.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Unpaid carers impacted by unclear guidance to have debts cancelled

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Hundreds of thousands of sick or disabled people will be offered voluntary help towards employment as part of a package of measures coming into force today (6 April) that will encourage work and save taxpayers around £1 billion.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Thousands to be supported into work as government reforms welfare system

details.secondary_corporate_information_pages: Read about the types of information we routinely publish in our Publication scheme. Find out about our commitment to publishing in Welsh. Our Personal information charter explains how we treat your personal information. Read our policy on Social media use.

details.body:
The Permanent Secretary is the head of the department, responsible for the effectiveness and efficiency of the department’s work and its spending.

They are also responsible for the leadership, management and staffing of the department.

role_appointments.title: Peter Schofield - Permanent Secretary

details.body:
The Finance Director General is responsible for:

  • the allocation of resources
  • financial management and control
  • commercial procurement and contract management
  • security and data protection
  • analysis and science
  • risk and resilience

role_appointments.title: Peter Schofield - Director General, Finance

details.body:
The MHCLG board’s role is to advise and support ministers on the operational implications and effectiveness of policy proposals and help turn policy from ideas into actions and results.

Part of MHCLG’s leadership team, and an executive member of its board, the Director General, Housing and Planning helps with that task.

role_appointments.title: Peter Schofield - Director General, Housing

details.body:
In his role as Director General, Fraud, Disability and Health Neil:

  • leads the Fraud Error and Debt (FED) Programme alongside FED operational delivery and policy 

  • leads the delivery of Disability Services  

  • is responsible for the Health Transformation Programme which will transform DWP’s operational delivery for disabled people and people with health conditions

As SRO for Universal Credit he is accountable to Parliament for implementing the government’s main welfare reform programme. This includes:

  • owning and communicating the vision of the programme
  • ensuring the implementation of Universal Credit is completed safely and securely
  • providing clear leadership to the programme team

role.title: Director General, Fraud, Disability and Health and Senior Responsible Owner Universal Credit

role_appointments.title: Neil Couling CB CBE - Director General, Fraud, Disability and Health and Senior Responsible Owner Universal Credit

details.body:
The Director General, DWP Services and Fraud:

  • leads the Fraud, Error and Debt (FED) Programme alongside FED operational delivery and policy  
  • leads the delivery of Disability Services   
  • is responsible for the Health Transformation Programme which will transform DWP’s operational delivery for disabled people and people with health conditions 
  • leads the delivery of services across the lifecycle of UK households, including child maintenance and retirement services

role.title: Director General, DWP Services and Fraud

  • ensuring the implementation of Universal Credit is completed safely and securely
  • providing clear leadership to the programme team

role_appointments.title: Neil Couling CBE - Director General, Universal Credit Programme

details.body:
The Director General, Corporate Transformation sets and leads the department’s workplace transformation and estates strategy, aligning this to business strategy and the wider Civil Service workforce plan. 

They also support the Permanent Secretary in their capacity as lead Accounting Officer on Synergy, the Shared Services Transformation Programme for DWP, Ministry of Justice, Home Office and Defra: a substantial change programme, hosted and sponsored in DWP on behalf of the 4 departments. 

The role is accountable for: 

  • Synergy Cluster – supporting delivery of this programme as DG sponsor 

  • Estates strategy 

  • Workplace Transformation Programme strategy and programme sponsor 

  • Government reform in DWP

The Director General (DG) and Chief Operating Officer (COO) for the Department for International Trade (DIT) oversees the finance, digital and technology, human resources and commercial functions.

The COO is member of the Departmental Board and Executive Committee and chairs the Performance, Finance and Risk Committee and the Projects and Change Committee.

details.body:
The Director of Finance and Business Services is responsible for the financial management of HM Land Registry and compliance with the general requirements of Government Accounting.

They oversee the preparation of our annual report and accounts and are responsible for ensuring the key performance indicator for efficiency is achieved. They provide advice to the Chief Executive and the Portfolio Board on the regularity and propriety of expenditure and also provide advice on commercial contracts.

details.body:
The Director General, Jobs and Careers Service Operations is responsible for:

  • the day to day to running of Universal Credit and working age operations across Great Britain via jobcentres and service centres
  • the provision of all working age face-to-face services through jobcentres, including the relationship with employers and other external partners
  • leading the transition to a new jobs and careers service, part of the government’s Get Britain Working plans

  • the day to day to running of Universal Credit and working age operations across Great Britain via jobcentres and service centres
  • the provision of all working age face-to-face services through jobcentres, including the relationship with employers and other external partners
  • the delivery of services across the lifecycle of UK households, including child maintenance and pensioners

  • the day to day to running of disability services, Universal Credit and working age operations across Great Britain via jobcentres and service centres
  • the provision of all working age face-to-face services through jobcentres, including the relationship with employers and other external partners
  • the delivery of services across the lifecycle of UK households, including child maintenance and pensioners

details.body:
The Director General, People and Capability sets and leads the department’s people strategy, aligning this to business strategy and the wider Civil Service workforce plan.

The role is accountable for:

  • the full HR operating model and function
  • health and safety
  • learning and development
  • strategic workforce planning
  • creating a flexible, inclusive and continuously learning workforce

role_appointments.title: Julie Blomley - Director General, People, Capability and Place

details.body:
The Chief People Officer works across the Home Office portfolio, and has overall responsibility for the following:

  • equality, inclusion and wellbeing
  • HR strategies
  • HR policies and legal
  • workforce transformation
  • organisational development and design
  • strategic business partnering
  • strategic workforce planning and resourcing
  • reward policies
  • cross government programmes and projects

details.body:
The Director General for Welfare and Pensions is responsible for: 

  • Poverty strategy, including departmental poverty policy and the Child Poverty Taskforce 

  • Leading DWP outcomes on health and disability

  • The Government’s overall social security system and welfare strategy, including DWP’s Annual Managed Expenditure position 

  • Oversight of key arm’s length bodies, including the Health and Safety Executive, Office for Nuclear Regulation, the Pensions Regulator, Pension Protection Fund, and NEST

role.title: Director General for Welfare and Pensions

details.body:
The Director General for Labour Market and Skills is responsible for: 

  • Development and implementation of labour market strategy and policy, including programmes to reduce inactivity and the Youth Guarantee, and devolution of employment support to local authorities 

  • Leading DWP outcomes in the labour market, ensuring a joined-up approach across work, health and skills

  • Adult Skills policy and implementation including Skills England, Apprenticeships, Adult Skills Funding, and post-19 careers

details.body:
The Chief Digital and Information Officer is responsible for: 

  • co-creating and evolving modern, secure, user-centred services 
  • designing and building technology and utilising data to deliver solutions 
  • stability and resilience of the department’s digital estate, which includes: 
  • core products for each service line (for example, Jobseeker’s Allowance Payment System, Pensions Service Computer System) 
  • common products which each interact with core products (for example, Customer Information System, Central Payments System) 
  • data collection, storage, linkage and analysis capability including automation and artificial intelligence 
  • shared infrastructure including desktops, telephony, data centres and networks

role_appointments.title: Helen Wylie - Chief Digital and Information Officer

  • leading DWP’s strategic transformation
  • owning and delivering the Department’s change portfolio
  • Designing and delivering DWP’s digital services
  • running DWP’s IT estate

role_appointments.title: Helen Wylie - Director General, Digital and Transformation

details.body:
Our non-executive directors are senior figures from outside the department who bring a diverse mix of expertise and skills from across the public and private sector. They all:

  • give guidance and advice to DWP leaders and ministers
  • support and challenge management on the department’s strategic direction
  • provide support in monitoring and reviewing progress

details.body: As Minister for Intergovernmental Relations, the role holder leads coordination with the devolved administrations on the Prime Minister’s behalf, working closely with the Territorial Offices, to make sure that across government work is being done on behalf of the entire United Kingdom: England, Northern Ireland, Scotland, and Wales.

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Pat McFadden MP - Minister for Intergovernmental Relations

details.body:
The Secretary of State has overall responsibility for the business of the department, including the Departmental Strategy, planning and performance, reporting and governance requirements. They have direct responsibility for departmental expenditure.

DWP is responsible for the administration of the State Pension and working age benefits system, providing support to:

  • people of working age
  • employers
  • pensioners
  • families and children
  • disabled people

role.title: Secretary of State for Work and Pensions

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Pat McFadden MP - Secretary of State for Work and Pensions

details.body:
The Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster administers the estates and rents of the Duchy of Lancaster, and is a member of the Cabinet.

He is responsible for:

  • Oversight of all Cabinet Office policy
  • Coordination and delivery of cross-government priorities
  • Oversight of national security policy coordination
  • Oversight of state reform and Civil Service reform
  • Communications policy for Government 
  • Propriety and ethics 
  • Public appointments

details.body: Lords spokesperson on equalities.

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Baroness Smith of Malvern - Government spokesperson for Equalities

details.body:
The minister’s responsibilities include:

  • lead policy responsibility for gender equality and women’s rights
  • sponsorship of the Equality and Human Rights Commission (EHRC) and Social Mobility Commission (SMC)
  • UK equality framework (including the Equality Act)
  • equality data and analysis

The minister also has lead responsibility in the House of Lords on the following policy areas:

  • socioeconomic opportunity
  • race and ethnicity policy
  • Employment Rights Bill (Department for Business and Trade lead)

role.title: Minister of State (Minister for Women and Equalities)

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Baroness Smith of Malvern - Minister of State (Minister for Women and Equalities)

details.body:
The minister’s Department for Education (DfE) responsibilities include:

  • governance, intervention and accountability of further education colleges
  • funding for education and training, provision and outcomes for 16- to 19-year-olds
  • further education financial stability and workforce
  • access to higher education, participation and lifelong learning entitlement
  • quality of higher education and the student experience (including the Office for Students)
  • student finance (including the Student Loans Company)
  • technical and vocational qualifications (level 3 and below), including T Levels
  • higher technical education (levels 4 and 5)
  • careers advice
  • support for young people aged 16 to 19 not in employment, education or training (NEET)
  • skills strategy and delivery
  • technical excellence colleges
  • international education

The minister’s Department for Work and Pensions (DWP) responsibilities include:

  • Skills England and Industry Training Boards
  • adult education, including basic skills
  • apprenticeships, including the growth and skills levy
  • skills bootcamps
  • sector skills packages
  • careers advice and support for young people over the age of 19 not in employment, education or training (NEET)
  • Youth Guarantee

details.body:
The Secretary of State has overall responsibility for the business of the department, including the Departmental Strategy, planning and performance, reporting and governance requirements. They have direct responsibility for departmental expenditure.

DWP is responsible for the administration of the State Pension and working age benefits system, providing support to:

  • people of working age
  • employers
  • pensioners
  • families and children
  • disabled people

ordered_roles.title: Secretary of State for Work and Pensions

details.body:
In July 2019 the role of Minister of State for Employment was replaced by the role of Parliamentary Under Secretary of State (Minister for Employment).

The minister’s responsibilities include:

  • Universal Credit, including labour market aspects and overall programme management
  • employment strategy and labour market interventions, including:
    • conditionality and sanctions
    • youth employment
    • women’s employment
    • black, Asian and minority ethnic employment
    • Fuller Working Lives
    • New Enterprise Allowance
  • Jobcentre Plus, partnership working and employer engagement
  • EU and international affairs, including support to the Secretary of State on Brexit
  • support to the Secretary of State on devolution

In July 2016 the role of Minister of State for Pensions was replaced by the role of Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Pensions.

The Minister of State for Pensions is responsible for:

  • pensions policy, state pensions and Pension Credit
  • private pensions, automatic enrolment and the National Employment Savings Trust (NEST)
  • The Pensions Regulator, Pension Protection Fund, Financial Assistance Scheme, The Pensions Advisory Service and the Pensions Ombudsman
  • Fuller Working Lives
  • Winter Fuel Payments and Cold Weather Payments
  • child maintenance

In July 2016 the role of Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Disabled People was replaced by the role of Minister of State for Disabled People, Health and Work.

The Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Disabled People is responsible for:

  • cross-government disability issues and strategy
  • disability benefits (Disability Living Allowance, Personal Independence Payment and Attendance Allowance)
  • mental health matters
  • Fit for Work
  • carers
  • appeals reform
  • Access to Work
  • Health and Safety Executive

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Disabled People

details.body:
The Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Welfare Reform is responsible for:

  • Universal Credit
  • Housing Benefit
  • benefit cap (a limit on the total amount of benefit people can get)
  • the Social Security Advisory Committee
  • credit unions
  • mental health review
  • the Health and Work Service
  • bereavement benefits
  • health and safety

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Welfare Reform

details.body:
The Permanent Secretary is the head of the department, responsible for the effectiveness and efficiency of the department’s work and its spending.

They are also responsible for the leadership, management and staffing of the department.

The Second Permanent Secretary and Chief Operating Officer is responsible for all the department’s services to the public, both those delivered by our own staff and those delivered under contract. They are also responsible for business continuity.

ordered_roles.title: Second Permanent Secretary and Chief Operating Officer

details.body:
The Director General, Professional Services, makes sure that the department’s policies are evidence-based, lawful and well-communicated. Professional Services includes:

  • communications
  • information, governance and security
  • DWP and Department of Health legal services
  • medical and analytical services
  • ministerial private office

The Director General is currently also the department’s:

  • senior lawyer
  • senior information risk owner
  • transparency champion

The Director General, Human Resources is responsible for the department’s human resources service.

The Head of Government HR Operations includes cross-government responsibility for the human resources profession and talent management.

ordered_roles.title: Director General, Human Resources and Head of Government HR Operations

details.body:
The Director General of Strategy advises and supports ministers and the Permanent Secretary on current and future policies including:

  • the labour market
  • welfare reform
  • pensions reform
  • social justice

The Director General, Operations is responsible for:

  • delivering public services for the Department for Work and Pensions
  • providing services to promote parental responsibilities (including family-based arrangements) and managing the statutory child maintenance scheme

The Information Technology (IT) Director General and Chief Information Officer (CIO) is responsible for the development and provision of the Department’s IT services, including those provided under contract.

ordered_roles.title: IT Director General and Chief Information Officer

details.body:
The Finance Director General is responsible for:

  • the allocation of resources
  • financial management and control
  • commercial procurement and contract management
  • security and data protection
  • analysis and science
  • risk and resilience

In May 2015 the role of Minister of State for Disabled People was replaced by the role of Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Disabled People.

The Minister of State for Disabled People is responsible for:

  • cross-government disability issues and strategy
  • Employment and Support Allowance, Work Capability Assessment and Incapacity Benefit Reassessment Programme
  • disability benefits (Disability Living Allowance, Personal Independence Payment and Attendance Allowance)
  • carers
  • appeals reform
  • fraud and error (including debt management)

ordered_roles.title: Minister of State for Disabled People

details.body:
The Director General, Business Transformation Group is responsible for:

  • the development and oversight of the department’s business transformation strategy
  • ensuring that we build services that are reusable, secure and efficient to maintain
  • building sustainable programme management and digital capability across the department
  • actively promoting and implementing the principles of equality, diversity and inclusion in his role as the department’s race champion

The Universal Credit Director General and Senior Responsible Owner is accountable for implementing the government’s main welfare reform programme. This includes:

  • owning and communicating the vision of the programme
  • ensuring the implementation of Universal Credit is completed safely and securely
  • providing clear leadership to the programme team

The Director General, Digital Technology is accountable for the strategy, maintenance, integrity, value for money and continuous improvement of Department for Work and Pensions (DWP) information technology (IT) services and systems.
The Director General, Human Resources (HR), sets the department’s HR strategy – aligning this to both the business strategy and Civil Service Reform.

The minister’s responsibilities include:

  • spokesperson for all DWP business in the Lords
  • Universal Credit
  • Universal Support
  • fraud, error and debt

ordered_roles.title: Minister of State for Welfare Reform

details.body:
The minister’s responsibilities include:

  • benefit cap implementation and benefit uprating
  • other social assistance, including housing, supported accommodation, Support for Mortgage Interest, Cold Weather Payments, Bereavement Benefit and funeral payments
  • child maintenance and relationship support
  • cross-DWP House of Commons spokesperson

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Welfare Delivery

details.body:
The minister’s responsibilities include:

  • pensioner benefits, including new State Pension, Winter Fuel Payments and Pension Credit
  • State Pension age review
  • credit unions
  • private and occupational pensions, including automatic enrolment and the National Employment Savings Trust (NEST)
  • oversight of pensions arms-length bodies, including the Pensions Regulator, Pension Protection Fund, Financial Assistance Scheme, Pensions Advisory Service and Pensions Ombudsman

The role of Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Pensions replaced the role of Minister of State for Pensions in July 2016.

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Pensions

details.body:
The Chief Digital and Information Officer is responsible for: 

  • co-creating and evolving modern, secure, user-centred services 
  • designing and building technology and utilising data to deliver solutions 
  • stability and resilience of the department’s digital estate, which includes: 
  • core products for each service line (for example, Jobseeker’s Allowance Payment System, Pensions Service Computer System) 
  • common products which each interact with core products (for example, Customer Information System, Central Payments System) 
  • data collection, storage, linkage and analysis capability including automation and artificial intelligence 
  • shared infrastructure including desktops, telephony, data centres and networks

The minister’s responsibilities include:

  • spokesperson for DWP business in the House of Lords
  • child maintenance and family test
  • parental conflict
  • legislation and statutory instruments strategy
  • Social Security Advisory Committee (SSAC) relationship management
  • Social Fund (Cold Weather Payments, Sure Start Maternity grants and Funeral Expenses Payment scheme)
  • bereavement benefits
  • maternity benefits
  • departmental business, including oversight of:
    • departmental capability in commercial and digital affairs
    • commercial contracting policy
    • resourcing and estates
    • transparency and data-sharing issues
    • research and trialling
  • Office for Nuclear Regulation

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State (Minister for Work and Pensions)

details.body:
The minister’s responsibilities include:

  • pensioner benefits, including new State Pension, Winter Fuel Payments, Pension Credit and Attendance Allowance
  • private and occupational pensions, including regulatory powers and the National Employment Savings Trust (NEST)
  • automatic enrolment into a workplace pension
  • oversight of arms-length bodies, including the Pensions Regulator, Pension Protection Fund, Financial Assistance Scheme and Pensions Ombudsman
  • financial guidance, budgeting, saving and debt, including the Money and Pensions Service and Financial Inclusion Policy Forum
  • methods of payment and Post Office Card Accounts
  • Net Zero
  • cross-DWP spokesperson – shadowing Lords

The role of Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Pensions and Financial Inclusion replaced the role of Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Pensions in June 2017.

The minister’s responsibilities include:

  • cross-DWP Commons spokesperson
  • workless families, relationship support and support for disadvantaged groups
  • childcare and maternity benefits
  • child maintenance
  • financial support for housing, including within Universal Credit
  • other social assistance, including supported accommodation, Support for Mortgage Interest, Cold Weather Payments, bereavement benefits and funeral payments
  • benefit cap implementation and benefit uprating

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Family Support, Housing and Child Maintenance

details.body:
The Director General, Universal Credit Operations is responsible for:

  • the day to day to running of Universal Credit operations across Great Britain via jobcentres and service centres
  • the provision of all working age face-to-face services through jobcentres, including the relationship with employers and other external partners

The Director General for Strategy, Policy and Analysis advises and supports ministers and the Permanent Secretary on current and future policies including:

  • the labour market
  • welfare reform
  • pensions reform
  • social justice

The role of Director General for Policy Group replaced the role of Director General for Strategy, Policy and Analysis in May 2018.

ordered_roles.title: Director General, Strategy, Policy and Analysis

details.body:
The Director General for Policy Group advises and supports ministers and the Permanent Secretary on current and future policies including:

  • the labour market
  • welfare reform
  • pensions reform
  • social justice

The role of Director General for Policy Group replaced the role of Director General for Strategy, Policy and Analysis in May 2018.

In his role as Director General, Fraud, Disability and Health Neil:

  • leads the Fraud Error and Debt (FED) Programme alongside FED operational delivery and policy 

  • leads the delivery of Disability Services  

  • is responsible for the Health Transformation Programme which will transform DWP’s operational delivery for disabled people and people with health conditions

As SRO for Universal Credit he is accountable to Parliament for implementing the government’s main welfare reform programme. This includes:

  • owning and communicating the vision of the programme
  • ensuring the implementation of Universal Credit is completed safely and securely
  • providing clear leadership to the programme team

ordered_roles.title: Director General, Fraud, Disability and Health and Senior Responsible Owner Universal Credit

details.body:
The Director General, People and Capability sets and leads the department’s people strategy, aligning this to business strategy and the wider Civil Service workforce plan.

The role is accountable for:

  • the full HR operating model and function
  • health and safety
  • learning and development
  • strategic workforce planning
  • creating a flexible, inclusive and continuously learning workforce

The Director General, Work and Health Services is responsible for:

  • the day to day to running of disability services, Universal Credit and working age operations across Great Britain via jobcentres and service centres
  • the provision of all working age face-to-face services through jobcentres, including the relationship with employers and other external partners
  • the delivery of services across the lifecycle of UK households, including child maintenance and pensioners

ordered_roles.title: Director General, Work and Health Services

details.body:
The Director General, Strategy and Transformation is responsible for:

  • The department’s business strategy, leading out and coordinating the transformation of our services to meet our 2030 ambitions 

  • Leadership of DWP’s Major Change Portfolio 

  • Delivering the overall objectives of the Major Change Portfolio

Amanda is also the Diversity and Inclusion Social Mobility Champion.

The minister’s responsibilities include:

  • overall management and delivery of Universal Credit
  • support for disadvantaged groups in Universal Credit including care leavers, prison leavers, survivors of domestic abuse, people with drug or alcohol dependency, rough sleepers and those who are facing homelessness
  • housing policy and Housing Benefit delivery, including Support for Mortgage Interest and supported accommodation
  • ‘Help to Claim’ service
  • poverty and cost of living
  • fraud, error and debt
  • benefit uprating
  • EU Exit oversight
  • Jobseeker’s Allowance and income support
  • benefit cap
  • Budgeting Loans

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State (Minister for Welfare Delivery)

details.body:
The Director General for Social Security, Disability and Pensions is responsible for: 

  • the development and implementation of strategy and policy to improve outcomes for people receiving support from the social security system; and in particular for disabled people, people with health conditions, and pensioners and savers 

  • the Government’s overall social security system and welfare strategy  

  • Universal Credit policy and analysis, Housing policy and analysis, and Carers policy and analysis  

  • other policy areas including devolution  

  • DWP’s partnerships with arm’s length bodies.

ordered_roles.title: Director General, Social Security, Disability and Pensions

details.body:
The Director General for Labour Market Policy and Implementation is responsible for:

  • the development and implementation of labour market policy, including the approach to post-Covid economic recovery
  • social security coordination and welfare policy including Universal Credit
  • in-work progression and poverty strategy

ordered_roles.title: Director General, Labour Market Policy and Implementation

details.body:
Our non-executive directors are senior figures from outside the department who bring a diverse mix of expertise and skills from across the public and private sector. They all:

  • give guidance and advice to DWP leaders and ministers

  • support and challenge management on the department’s strategic direction

  • provide support in monitoring and reviewing progress

Our non-executive directors are senior figures from outside the department who bring a diverse mix of expertise and skills from across the public and private sector. They all:

  • give guidance and advice to DWP leaders and ministers
  • support and challenge management on the department’s strategic direction
  • provide support in monitoring and reviewing progress

Our non-executive directors are senior figures from outside the department who bring a diverse mix of expertise and skills from across the public and private sector. They all:

  • give guidance and advice to DWP leaders and ministers
  • support and challenge management on the department’s strategic direction
  • provide support in monitoring and reviewing progress

Our non-executive directors are senior figures from outside the department who bring a diverse mix of expertise and skills from across the public and private sector. They all:

  • give guidance and advice to DWP leaders and ministers
  • support and challenge management on the department’s strategic direction
  • provide support in monitoring and reviewing progress

Our non-executive directors are senior figures from outside the department who bring a diverse mix of expertise and skills from across the public and private sector. They all:

  • give guidance and advice to DWP leaders and ministers
  • support and challenge management on the department’s strategic direction
  • provide support in monitoring and reviewing progress

Our non-executive directors are senior figures from outside the department who bring a diverse mix of expertise and skills from across the public and private sector. They all:

  • give guidance and advice to DWP leaders and ministers
  • support and challenge management on the department’s strategic direction
  • provide support in monitoring and reviewing progress

The Universal Credit Programme Board Chair:

  • provides external advice, expertise and challenge, to inform the decision making process for delivering the Universal Credit programme
  • leads and facilitates discussions at Programme Board meetings ensuring decisions are made appropriately
  • gives advice on the operational and delivery implications of Universal Credit policy and plans proposals
  • provides constructive, independent support, guidance and robust challenge to the board on the progress and implementation of the programme plan
  • provides management advice to the DWP Permanent Secretary and Universal Credit senior responsible officer
  • ensures integrity of financial information and that financial controls and systems and risk management are robust and defensible

Responsibilities include:

  • labour market and in work progression
  • overall management and delivery of Universal Credit
  • Jobcentre Plus
  • in work conditionality and sanctions
  • Youth Offer
  • legacy benefits
  • poverty and Cost of Living
  • support for disadvantaged groups
  • international
  • methods of payment, including Post Office Card Accounts

ordered_roles.title: Minister of State (Minister for Work and Welfare)

details.body:
Responsibilities include:

  • pensioner benefits, including new State Pension, Winter Fuel Payments, Pension Credit and Attendance Allowance
  • private and occupational pensions, including regulatory powers and the National Employment Savings Trust (NEST)
  • cross-cutting role on economic growth, supporting MoS on the labour market
  • fraud, error and debt
  • housing policy and Housing Benefit delivery, including Support for Mortgage Interest and supported accommodation
  • Social Fund (Cold Weather Payments, Sure Start Maternity grants and Funeral Expenses Payment scheme)
  • oversight of arms-length bodies, including the Pensions Regulator, Pension Protection Fund, Financial Assistance Scheme and Pensions Ombudsman
  • Net Zero

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State (Minister for Pensions and Growth)

details.body:
Responsibilities include:

  • disability policy
  • oversight of Disability Unit, and convenor of Disability Champions
  • Work and Health strategy, including sponsorship of the joint Department for Work and Pensions / Department for Health and Social Care Work and Health Unit, and disability benefit reform
  • Disability Employment
  • Employment and Support Allowance (ESA), Personal Independence Payment (PIP), Disability Living Allowance (DLA) and supporting Minister of State on legacy benefits
  • Carer’s Allowance
  • Devolution Framework
  • Health and Safety Executive
  • Serious Case Panel
  • cross DWP Spokesperson – Shadowing Lords

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State (Minister for Disabled People, Health and Work)

details.body:
The minister’s responsibilities include:

  • responsibility for departmental strategy on the labour market, unemployment and in work progression, with a focus on under-represented groups, young people and skills
  • in-work conditionality including sanctions
  • international labour market policy (International Labour Organization (ILO), G20, Employment, Social Policy, Health and Consumer Affairs Council (EPSCO))
  • European Social Fund (ESF) and UK Shared Prosperity Fund (UKSPF)
  • work services and Jobcentre Plus partnership working
  • Jobcentre Plus campaigns
  • Jobseeker’s Allowance, Income Support
  • People and Location Programme
  • Youth Offer
  • Flexible Support Fund
  • labour market interventions for self-employment (including the New Enterprise Allowance and future offer and the Minimum Income Floor)
  • benefit cap
  • Health and Safety Executive

The role of Parliamentary Under Secretary of State (Minister for Employment) replaced the role Minister of State for Employment in July 2019.

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State (Minister for Employment)

details.body:
Responsibilities include:

  • spokesperson for DWP business in the House of Lords
  • child maintenance and family test
  • parental conflict
  • Bereavement Benefits
  • Maternity Benefits
  • coordination of departmental legislation
  • Social Security Advisory Committee (SSAC) relationship management
  • departmental business, including oversight of:
    • departmental capability in commercial and digital affairs
    • commercial contracting policy
    • resourcing and estates
    • transparency and data-sharing issues
    • research and trialling
  • Office for Nuclear Regulation

Responsibilities include:

  • support for disadvantaged groups
  • Youth Offer
  • childcare
  • Government Equalities Office (GEO) lead, Women and the Menopause
  • Military Covenant
  • poverty and cost of living
  • Housing Benefit strategy and delivery, including Support for Mortgage Interest and supported accommodation
  • Health & Safety Executive

details.body:
• Labour Market – including employer engagement 

  • Jobcentre Plus/National Jobs and Careers Service reform 

  • poverty 

  • addressing economic inactivity 

  • locally led employment support

  • Disability employment

  • Conditionality/sanctions

• Private Pensions 

  • State Pension

  • Social Fund 

  • Net Zero 

  • ALBs: Money and Pensions Service (MaPS), National Employment Savings Trust (NEST), The Pensions Ombudsman (TPO), Pensions Protection Fund (PPF), The Pensions Regulator (TPR) 

  • HM Treasury (HMT): Budget, Pension tax policy, Pensions freedoms policy, Pensions investment review, Public sector pensions

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State (Minister for Pensions)

details.body:
• DWP Lords spokesperson
  • departmental oversight including Commercial, Estates and Research
  • legislation coordination
  • disadvantaged groups
  • Shadow lead on Fraud legislation in the House of Lords
  • Social Security Advisory Committee oversight
  • Child Maintenance, Family Test and Reducing Parental Conflict

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State (Minister for Lords)

details.body:
The Director General for Labour Market Policy and Implementation is responsible for:

The Director General for Labour Market and Poverty is responsible for: 

  • Development and implementation of labour market strategy and policy 

  • Poverty strategy, including departmental poverty policy and the Child Poverty Taskforce 

  • Families, Child Maintenance and Disadvantaged Groups policy

ordered_roles.title: Director General for Labour Market and Poverty (jobshare)

details.body:
The Director General for Labour Market and Poverty is responsible for: 

  • Development and implementation of labour market strategy and policy 

  • Poverty strategy, including departmental poverty policy and the Child Poverty Taskforce 

  • Families, Child Maintenance and Disadvantaged Groups policy

ordered_roles.title: Director General for Labour Market and Poverty (jobshare)

details.body:
Our non-executive directors are senior figures from outside the department who bring a diverse mix of expertise and skills from across the public and private sector. They all:

  • give guidance and advice to DWP leaders and ministers
  • support and challenge management on the department’s strategic direction
  • provide support in monitoring and reviewing progress

Our non-executive directors are senior figures from outside the department who bring a diverse mix of expertise and skills from across the public and private sector. They all:

  • give guidance and advice to DWP leaders and ministers
  • support and challenge management on the department’s strategic direction
  • provide support in monitoring and reviewing progress

The Director General, Corporate Transformation sets and leads the department’s workplace transformation and estates strategy, aligning this to business strategy and the wider Civil Service workforce plan. 

They also support the Permanent Secretary in their capacity as lead Accounting Officer on Synergy, the Shared Services Transformation Programme for DWP, Ministry of Justice, Home Office and Defra: a substantial change programme, hosted and sponsored in DWP on behalf of the 4 departments. 

The role is accountable for: 

  • Synergy Cluster – supporting delivery of this programme as DG sponsor 

  • Estates strategy 

  • Workplace Transformation Programme strategy and programme sponsor 

  • Government reform in DWP

Responsibilities include:

  • pensioner benefits, including new State Pension, Winter Fuel Payments, Pension Credit and Attendance Allowance
  • private and occupational pensions, including regulatory powers and the National Employment Savings Trust (NEST)
  • Social Fund (Cold Weather Payments, Sure Start Maternity grants and Funeral Expenses Payment scheme)
  • oversight of arms-length bodies, including the Pensions Regulator, Pension Protection Fund, Financial Assistance Scheme and Pensions Ombudsman, and Office for Nuclear Regulation
  • methods of payment, including Post Office Card Account
  • Net Zero
  • Shadow Lords Minister

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State (Minister for Pensions)

details.body:
In April 2024 the role of Parliamentary Under Secretary of State (Minister for Disabled People, Health and Work) was replaced by the role of Minister of State (Minister for Disabled People, Health and Work).

Responsibilities include:

  • disability policy and cross-government responsibility for disabled people
  • oversight of Disability Unit, and convenor of Disability Champions
  • work and health strategy, including sponsorship of the joint Department for Work and Pensions / Department for Health and Social Care Work and Health Unit, and disability benefit reform
  • disability employment, and disability employment programmes
  • financial support for those at risk of falling out of work, and disabled claimants including, Disability Living Allowance (DLA), Personal Independence Payment (PIP), Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) and Carer’s Allowance (CA)
  • Social Mobility
  • Support for disadvantaged groups
  • Youth Offer
  • Government Equalities Office (GEO) lead, Women and the Menopause
  • Military Covenant
  • Housing Benefit strategy and delivery, including Support for Mortgage Interest and supported accommodation

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State (Minister for Disabled People, Health and Work)

details.body:
• Disability policy and x-Gov responsibility for disabled people 

  • Health and disability reform 

  • Universal Credit and Legacy Benefits Delivery 

  • Contributory Benefits, Personal Independence Payment (PIP), Disability Living Allowance (DLA), and Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) 

  • Carer’s Allowance (CA) 

  • Housing 

  • Access to Work 

  • ALB: Health and Safety Executive 

  • Serious Case Panel 

  • Uprating and Benefit Cap 

  • Oversight of Disability Unit (DU)

ordered_roles.title: Minister of State (Minister for Social Security and Disability)

details.body:
• Fraud, Error and Debt 

  • Digital, AI and Service Modernisation 

  • Devolution (National) 

  • International 

  • Customer Experience 

  • Estates 

  • Shadow Lords (Skills/Youth, CMS and Disadvantaged Groups) 

  • Synergy 

  • Deputy for Ministers of State / Legislation in Parliament 

  • DWP Resilience 

  • ALBs: Industrial Injuries Advisory Council (IIAC) and the Office for Nuclear Regulation (ONR)

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State (Minister for Transformation)

details.body:
Responsibilities include:

  • disability policy and cross-government responsibility for disabled people
  • oversight of Disability Unit, and convenor of Disability Champions
  • work and health strategy, including sponsorship of the joint Department for Work and Pensions / Department for Health and Social Care Work and Health Unit, and disability benefit reform
  • disability employment, and disability employment programmes
  • financial support for those at risk of falling out of work, and disabled claimants including, Disability Living Allowance (DLA), Personal Independence Payment (PIP), Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) and Carer’s Allowance (CA)
  • Support for disadvantaged groups
  • Youth Offer
  • Government Equalities Office (GEO) lead, Women and the Menopause
  • Military Covenant
  • Housing Benefit strategy and delivery, including Support for Mortgage Interest and supported accommodation

Between December 2023 and April 2024 this role was carried out by the Parliamentary Under Secretary of State (Minister for Disabled People, Health and Work).

ordered_roles.title: Minister of State (Minister for Disabled People, Health and Work)

details.body:
The Director General, Operations is responsible for:

  • the day to day to running of Universal Credit and working age operations across Great Britain via jobcentres and service centres
  • the provision of all working age face-to-face services through jobcentres, including the relationship with employers and other external partners
  • the delivery of services across the lifecycle of UK households, including child maintenance and pensioners

• DWP Lords spokesperson 

  • Departmental oversight including Commercial and Research 

  • Legislation Coordination 

  • Disadvantaged Groups 

  • Shadow Fraud in the House of Lords 

  • Social Security Advisory Committee (SSAC) Oversight 

  • Child Maintenance, Family Test and Reducing Parental Conflict

The Director General, DWP Services and Fraud:

  • leads the Fraud, Error and Debt (FED) Programme alongside FED operational delivery and policy  
  • leads the delivery of Disability Services   
  • is responsible for the Health Transformation Programme which will transform DWP’s operational delivery for disabled people and people with health conditions 
  • leads the delivery of services across the lifecycle of UK households, including child maintenance and retirement services

ordered_roles.title: Director General, DWP Services and Fraud

details.body:
The minister’s Department for Education (DfE) responsibilities include:

  • governance, intervention and accountability of further education colleges
  • funding for education and training, provision and outcomes for 16- to 19-year-olds
  • further education financial stability and workforce
  • access to higher education, participation and lifelong learning entitlement
  • quality of higher education and the student experience (including the Office for Students)
  • student finance (including the Student Loans Company)
  • technical and vocational qualifications (level 3 and below), including T Levels
  • higher technical education (levels 4 and 5)
  • careers advice
  • support for young people aged 16 to 19 not in employment, education or training (NEET)
  • skills strategy and delivery
  • technical excellence colleges
  • international education

The minister’s Department for Work and Pensions (DWP) responsibilities include:

  • Skills England and Industry Training Boards
  • adult education, including basic skills
  • apprenticeships, including the growth and skills levy
  • skills bootcamps
  • sector skills packages
  • careers advice and support for young people over the age of 19 not in employment, education or training (NEET)
  • Youth Guarantee

• Labour Market – including employer engagement 

  • Jobs and Careers Service reform/Jobcentre Plus, including post-19 careers 

  • Poverty 

  • Work and Health: Occupational Health and Statutory Sick Pay 

  • Locally led employment support 

  • Conditionality and Sanctions 

  • Childcare and Parental Employment 

  • In Work Progression (conditionality and careers) 

  • Devolution (Local) 

  • Disability Employment

The Director General for Welfare and Pensions is responsible for: 

  • Poverty strategy, including departmental poverty policy and the Child Poverty Taskforce 

  • Leading DWP outcomes on health and disability

  • The Government’s overall social security system and welfare strategy, including DWP’s Annual Managed Expenditure position 

  • Oversight of key arm’s length bodies, including the Health and Safety Executive, Office for Nuclear Regulation, the Pensions Regulator, Pension Protection Fund, and NEST

ordered_roles.title: Director General for Welfare and Pensions

details.body:
The Director General for Labour Market and Skills is responsible for: 

  • Development and implementation of labour market strategy and policy, including programmes to reduce inactivity and the Youth Guarantee, and devolution of employment support to local authorities 

  • Leading DWP outcomes in the labour market, ensuring a joined-up approach across work, health and skills

  • Adult Skills policy and implementation including Skills England, Apprenticeships, Adult Skills Funding, and post-19 careers

ordered_roles.title: Director General for Labour Market and Skills

details.body:
The Director General, Jobs and Careers Service Operations is responsible for:

  • the day to day to running of Universal Credit and working age operations across Great Britain via jobcentres and service centres
  • the provision of all working age face-to-face services through jobcentres, including the relationship with employers and other external partners
  • leading the transition to a new jobs and careers service, part of the government’s Get Britain Working plans

ordered_roles.title: Chief Executive and Director General, Jobs and Careers Service Operations

details.body:
The Director General, Digital and Transformation is responsible for:

  • leading DWP’s strategic transformation
  • owning and delivering the Department’s change portfolio
  • Designing and delivering DWP’s digital services
  • running DWP’s IT estate

title: Department for Work and Pensions
